title: Extract workspace-sync Step 4c into monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js helper — eliminates LLM-skip-by-omission and cross-bash-session PULL_MOVED loss; always writes a sentinel (no silent early-return).
author: tinker
source_directive: DIRECTIVE-20260531-002 (operator filed HIGH at 05:21Z after observing that PROP-064's workstream-A+B fixes are present in workspace-sync.md but Step 4c remains effectively dead in production — confirmed by zero new sync-workspace-runs sentinels for ~28h after the bootstrap and by tinker-2026-05-31T02-40 FND-01 needing to manually rescue 5 stale FUSE files).
executive_summary: PROP-064 shipped two structural fixes (PRE_PULL_SHA capture + four-way classification) but the post-deployment record still shows the silent-skip pattern: ONE sync-workspace-runs success sentinel exists (the 2026-05-30T01:16:37Z bootstrap that landed PROP-064 itself); ONE Step 4c sentinel commit exists (`ea413b4` for the 2026-05-30T17:11:28Z cycle, which was class=equal/pull_moved=false/action=no-sync-needed — a custom action not in the prompt schema); ZERO further Step 4c artifacts across the next ~18 workspace-sync cycles despite confirmed upstream commits from decider/integrity/poller landing between many of those cycles (e.g. the 2026-05-31T01:20Z cycle had integrity@01:15Z + poller@00:07Z + workspace-sync@00:08Z between pulls — PULL_MOVED should have been 1; no sentinel written). PROP-064's design was correct; the gap is execution. Two failure modes are at work and both must be closed: (1) cross-bash-session variable loss — Step 1's `PRE_PULL_SHA` / `POST_PULL_SHA` / `PULL_MOVED` are set in one MCP `bash` tool call and read in Step 4c's separate `bash` tool call; bash environment does not survive across tool calls; PULL_MOVED is therefore always unset (effectively 0) when Step 4c reads it, suppressing the upstream-moved trigger that PROP-064 workstream A was supposed to add. (2) LLM skip-by-omission — Step 4c's bash block is ~140 lines of prose-interleaved logic two-thirds of the way through a 1370-line prompt; the Haiku agent running workspace-sync is observably stopping at Step 4b's commit and moving directly to Step 5's report without invoking the Step 4c block (zero Step 4c commit messages across most cycles confirms this). Fix: extract Step 4c into a 60-80 line Node helper at `monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js` that derives PULL_MOVED *internally* from the timestamp of the most recent `monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json` sentinel (vs. the latest origin/main commit timestamp), removes any dependency on Step-1-set bash variables, ALWAYS writes either a sync-runs sentinel OR a divergence audit (no silent early-return), and is invoked from workspace-sync.md via a single 3-line bash block at the end of Step 4. The single-line invocation eliminates skip-by-omission (it is structurally obvious as a numbered action, not a 140-line block buried in prose). Idempotent on success. Aligns with the precedent set by `monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js` (PROP-050), `monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js` (PROP-041 Phase 2), and `monitor/scripts/prune-integrity.js` (PROP-051 Workstream C) — all the structural patches of the past 2 weeks have moved complex bash out of prompts and into scripts.

post_prop_064_evidence.sync_runs_sentinel_count_since_deploymen: ONE — `sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-30T01-16-37Z.json` (the bootstrap cycle that committed PROP-064). Subsequent cycles: zero.
post_prop_064_evidence.step_4c_commit_messages_since_deployment: ONE — `ea413b4 PROP-061 git→FUSE detect+sync: 2026-05-30T17:11:28Z`. That single commit's divergence sentinel shows classification=equal, pull_moved=false, action=no-sync-needed. action='no-sync-needed' is NOT in the prompt schema (valid values: 'auto-sync', 'abort-non-ff', 'benign-local-ahead') — the LLM was improvising. Every other cycle wrote no Step 4c commit at all.
post_prop_064_evidence.cycle_2026_05_31T01_20Z_proof_of_pull_mo: Between the 2026-05-31T00:08:55Z workspace-sync and the 2026-05-31T01:20:18Z workspace-sync, two upstream commits landed: `755f1a2 integrity: 2026-05-31T01-15Z — FAIL` and `ae47bd7 poller: 2026-05-31T00:07Z — quiet`. The 01:20Z cycle's Step 1 pull would have absorbed both — PRE_PULL_SHA != POST_PULL_SHA — setting PULL_MOVED=1. PROP-064 workstream A specifically targeted this case. Yet no Step 4c sentinel exists for the 01:20Z cycle (no git-to-fuse-divergence-2026-05-31T01*.json, no sync-workspace-runs-2026-05-31T01*.json). Either Step 4c didn't run, or it ran and took the silent early-return at workspace-sync.md:1232.
post_prop_064_evidence.agent_notes_silence: Spot-check of 8 recent workspace-sync run reports (2026-05-30T21:08 through 2026-05-31T05:18): none of their agent_notes fields mention Step 4c, PROP-064, classification, sync-workspace, or git→FUSE. Each one mentions Step 2 skip statistics and Step 4a commit counts in detail. The asymmetry is consistent with Step 4c not being invoked at all in those cycles.
post_prop_064_evidence.manual_rescue_evidence: tinker-2026-05-31T02-40 FND-01 found 5 stale FUSE files (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, docs/index.html, monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json) and manually ran `node build.js sync-workspace` to recover them. The target itself is healthy; the issue is exclusively in workspace-sync's failure to invoke it.

failure_mode_1_cross_bash_session_variab.summary: PROP-064 workstream A sets PRE_PULL_SHA / POST_PULL_SHA / PULL_MOVED inside Step 1's bash block (workspace-sync.md lines 149-157). Step 4c reads `${PULL_MOVED:-0}` at lines 1226 and 1259. The MCP bash tool spawns a fresh shell for each tool call — environment variables, exported or not, do NOT persist between calls. The LLM agent running workspace-sync invokes Step 1 as one bash call and Step 4c as a separate bash call (the prompt structure of `### Step 1` ... ```bash ... ``` ... `### Step 2` ... etc. naturally invites this). Result: PULL_MOVED is always unset (treated as 0) when Step 4c reads it. The `[ "${PULL_MOVED:-0}" = "1" ] && NEED_SYNC=1` clause never fires, suppressing the upstream-moved trigger entirely.
failure_mode_1_cross_bash_session_variab.fires_when: Every workspace-sync cycle that has upstream commits landed between cycles. Estimated rate: ~60-80% of cycles based on integrity+poller+decider commit frequency.
failure_mode_1_cross_bash_session_variab.PROP_064_assumed_otherwise: PROP-064's workstream A design assumed bash variable persistence (no mention of tmpfile or other cross-session mechanism). The Operational Notes section of workspace-sync.md actually documents the same gotcha for SKIP_LOG (see line 168 `mktemp the SKIP_LOG` and line 169 `No EXIT trap on the SKIP_LOG`), but PROP-064 did not apply the same persistence pattern to PULL_MOVED.

failure_mode_2_llm_skip_by_omission.summary: Step 4c is a ~140-line bash block buried between Step 4b's run-report push (line 1185) and Step 5's one-line report (line 1345). The block is preceded by 13 lines of section heading + safety architecture bullets + the 'PROP-064 four-way classification' explanatory paragraph. When the Haiku agent reaches Step 4b's commit and sees the workflow's apparent completion (commit pushed, run report written), it tends to skip directly to Step 5's `Output a one-line summary` line. The Step 4c block does not begin with a strongly imperative cue like 'Run this NOW' or a numbered list item; it begins with a section heading and prose. Skip rate observed: ~94% of cycles since PROP-064 deployment write no Step 4c artifact.
failure_mode_2_llm_skip_by_omission.fires_when: Every cycle. The skip is not data-conditional.
failure_mode_2_llm_skip_by_omission.evidence_distinguishing_mode_1_vs_mode_2: If failure_mode_1 were the only issue, we'd expect ALL cycles to have Step 4c commit messages with classification=equal (since PULL_MOVED=0 always, but Step 4c would still run and take the early-return). We see ZERO such commits across most cycles. The single 17:11Z commit (which improvised action='no-sync-needed') suggests the LLM sometimes runs Step 4c but doesn't always. Both failure modes are at work.

root_cause_diagnosis.interaction_with_secondary_bug: Even when Step 4c IS invoked and reaches the early-return at line 1232 (CLASSIFICATION=equal AND NEED_SYNC=0), the early-return writes NO sentinel. There is no record that Step 4c ran and decided 'no sync needed'. This makes failure_mode_2 indistinguishable from failure_mode_1+early-return-taken in the audit log. The fix must always write a sentinel (success, divergence audit, OR no-op-with-reason) so future tinker runs can distinguish 'didn't run' from 'ran-and-correctly-decided-no-sync'.

alternatives_considered.name: Persist PULL_MOVED via tmpfile (directive Step 2a recommendation)
alternatives_considered.summary: Minimal change: Step 1 writes `$PULL_MOVED` to a tmpfile (e.g. `mktemp -t workspace-sync-pull-moved.XXXXXX`); Step 4c reads it back. Same pattern as SKIP_LOG persistence at workspace-sync.md:178.
pros: Smallest possible change — ~6 lines added to Step 1, ~3 lines added to Step 4c.
Aligns with existing SKIP_LOG precedent — no new mechanism.
cons: Does nothing for failure_mode_2 (LLM skipping Step 4c entirely). If Step 4c isn't invoked, PULL_MOVED tmpfile is never read.
Does nothing for the silent-early-return bug — Step 4c still writes no sentinel when classification=equal AND PULL_MOVED=0.
Defers the deeper structural issue. Likely produces a fourth directive in 1-2 weeks when staleness recurs.
alternatives_considered.verdict: Insufficient. Does only 1 of the 3 required fixes.

alternatives_considered.name: Extract Step 4c into a monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js helper (RECOMMENDED)
alternatives_considered.summary: Write a 60-80 line Node helper that does everything Step 4c currently does: derive PULL_MOVED internally (from comparing the most recent `sync-workspace-runs-*.json` sentinel's timestamp to the latest origin/main commit timestamp, NOT from a bash variable); do the four-way LOCAL/REMOTE classification; write EITHER a sync-runs sentinel OR a divergence audit OR a no-op sentinel — always one of the three; commit + push the sentinel. Replace the entire 140-line Step 4c block in workspace-sync.md with a 3-line invocation: `node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js`.
pros: Closes failure_mode_1: no bash variables to lose; the script computes PULL_MOVED equivalent itself from durable on-disk state (last sentinel timestamp vs git log).

Closes failure_mode_2: a single 3-line bash invocation is structurally hard to skip; the LLM reads it as 'one more step before Step 5', not as '140 lines I can skim'. Mirrors the proven precedent of `node monitor/scripts/push-via-api.js`, `node monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js`, `node monitor/scripts/prune-integrity.js`.

Closes the silent-early-return bug: the script always writes a sentinel — `sync-workspace-runs-*.json` for sync-fired cycles, `git-to-fuse-divergence-*.json` for benign-divergence and non-FF cycles, and a new `sync-workspace-noop-*.json` (or extension of the divergence schema with action='no-op-no-upstream-movement') for the equal+no-pull case. Future tinker audits can distinguish 'ran and correctly no-op'd' from 'didn't run'.
Centralizes complex logic — easier to unit-test, easier to audit, easier to amend.
Reduces workspace-sync.md by ~130 lines (1370 → ~1240) — improves the prompt's readability and reduces Haiku context-load.
cons: Larger initial patch — ~80 lines new script + ~140 lines removed from prompt + ~5 lines new prompt block. Total LOC delta is small but the new-file requires a code review on the helper logic.

If the helper script has a bug, it fails in a single call (no partial fallback). Mitigation: the script's first action writes a 'starting' sentinel so even crashes leave a footprint; the EXIT trap in workspace-sync.md's bash still cleans the clone.
Adds another script under monitor/scripts/ to maintain. Already 3 there (audit-rewrite.js, push-via-api.js, prune-integrity.js), so the maintenance pattern is established.
alternatives_considered.verdict: RECOMMENDED. Closes all three sub-issues with one structural change; aligns with the existing helper-script pattern; reduces prompt size; centralizes auditable logic.

alternatives_considered.name: Step 4c execution-audit counter (directive Step 2c recommendation)
alternatives_considered.summary: Add a one-line `echo` at the end of Step 4c that increments a counter file (e.g. `monitor/integrity/step-4c-execution-counter.txt`). Tinker checks counter every run; if not incrementing, knows Step 4c isn't running.
pros: Minimal change — 2-3 lines added to Step 4c.
cons: Detector only — doesn't fix anything. Still leaves Step 4c skipped most cycles.
If the LLM skips Step 4c, it also skips the counter increment. So the counter doesn't increment, but the operator just confirms what tinker already knew from the missing sentinels.
alternatives_considered.verdict: Rejected as primary fix. Could be added as a tertiary belt-and-suspenders alongside option_2, but the helper script's own 'always write a sentinel' design already makes execution observable, so this is YAGNI.

alternatives_considered.name: Merge Step 4c invocation into Step 4b's bash block (eliminate cross-bash-session)
alternatives_considered.summary: Move the Step 4c bash block to immediately after Step 4b's commit, all in one bash invocation. PULL_MOVED set in Step 1 is still lost (Step 1 is a separate bash from Step 4b/4c), but at least Step 4c always runs.
pros: Closes failure_mode_2 partially (Step 4c runs whenever Step 4b runs).
cons: Doesn't close failure_mode_1 (PULL_MOVED still lost from Step 1).
Couples Step 4b's run-report write to Step 4c's sync — failures in Step 4c may roll back Step 4b's commit due to shared bash state.
Makes the Step 4 prompt block ~200 lines long, harder for the LLM to parse, may itself become skipable for different reasons.
alternatives_considered.verdict: Rejected. Marginal improvement, doesn't close root causes.

recommendation.rationale: It's the only option that closes ALL three failure surfaces (cross-session PULL_MOVED loss, LLM skip-by-omission, silent early-return), and it does so via a single structural change that aligns with the existing helper-script precedent. Options 1 and 3 are tactical patches that defer the underlying problem; option 4 is a non-fix. The helper-script extraction additionally reduces workspace-sync.md from 1370 to ~1240 lines, slightly improving Haiku context load (the agent is currently at the edge of its effective context for this prompt — supports the case that prompt size is a contributor to skip-by-omission).
recommendation.interaction_with_prop_064: PROP-064's workstream B (four-way classification) and workstream C (structure-integrity FUSE-staleness audit) remain valid and remain in their respective files. This PROP supersedes only workstream A's prompt-bash implementation. The four-way classification logic moves wholesale into the helper script. The structure-integrity audit at workstream C continues to be the secondary detector — independent canary that catches helper-script regressions if PROP-066 itself has a latent bug.
recommendation.interaction_with_prop_065: PROP-065's GIT_APPEND_ONLY classification deny-list remains active. The helper script does NOT need to know about that — it operates on origin/main HEAD comparisons, not per-file pushes. Independent fixes that compose cleanly.
recommendation.deprecation_note_for_prop_064: After PROP-066 deploys, PROP-064's workstream A code in Step 1 (PRE_PULL_SHA / POST_PULL_SHA / PULL_MOVED capture, workspace-sync.md lines 143-157) becomes dead code — the helper script ignores those variables and derives its own equivalent. Workstream B's four-way classification bash block in Step 4c becomes dead code — the helper script implements the same classification in Node. Recommend deleting both during PROP-066 deployment to avoid confusion (single source of truth).

responsibilities: Detect the most recent successful sync-workspace-runs sentinel timestamp (fallback to PROP-064-deployment-date 2026-05-30 if none).

Run `git fetch origin main` (silent) then `git log origin/main --since=<last-sentinel-ts> --format=%H | wc -l` to count upstream commits since last sync — equivalent to PULL_MOVED detection but derived from durable state, not bash variables.
Read LOCAL_SHA = `git rev-parse HEAD`, REMOTE_SHA = `git rev-parse origin/main`.
Compute four-way classification (equal / local-ancestor-of-remote / remote-ancestor-of-local / true-non-ff) using `git merge-base --is-ancestor`.
- classification=equal AND NEED_SYNC=0 → write `monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json` with `action: 'no-op-no-upstream-movement'`, files_copied:0, exit

- classification=local-ancestor-of-remote AND NEED_SYNC=1 → `git merge --ff-only origin/main`; spawn `node build.js sync-workspace`; write `sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json` with action='auto-sync' + files_copied + new_files
- classification=equal AND NEED_SYNC=1 (PULL_MOVED-equivalent fired) → no merge needed; spawn `node build.js sync-workspace`; write `sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json` with action='pull-moved-auto-sync'
- classification=remote-ancestor-of-local AND NEED_SYNC=0 → write `git-to-fuse-divergence-<ts>.json` with action='benign-local-ahead'
Stage + commit + push the sentinel file via the same `git commit -m 'PROP-061 git→FUSE detect+sync: <ts>'` pattern from existing Step 4c.
Exit 0 in all non-abort cases; exit 1 on abort (caller logs but does not propagate).
file_1_new_helper_script.no_external_deps: Pure Node stdlib + git CLI. No npm install needed.
file_1_new_helper_script.error_handling: On any throw, write `monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-step4c-crash-<ts>.json` with stack trace. Exit 2. Caller bash continues (the script's failure does not abort workspace-sync).
file_1_new_helper_script.first_action_sentinel: First line of script writes a stub `monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-step4c-started-<ts>.json` so even crashes leave a footprint. Cleared on normal exit (overwritten by the real sentinel). Detect crashes via stub-without-final-sentinel.

file_2_workspace_sync_md_changes.removed_lines: Lines 1187-1343 (Step 4c heading through closing `fi`). Approximately 157 lines removed.
file_2_workspace_sync_md_changes.added_lines:
Replaced with the following 8-line block at the same location:

```
### Step 4c: git→FUSE propagation

Invoke the helper script. It encapsulates the classification + sync logic from PROP-061/064/066. The script always writes a sentinel under monitor/integrity/ — inspect those for cycle audit, do not parse this step's stdout for state.

```bash
node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js 2>&1 | tail -5
# Commit the sentinel the script wrote, if any.
git add monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json monitor/integrity/git-to-fuse-divergence-*.json monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-*.json monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-step4c-crash-*.json 2>/dev/null || true
if ! git diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
  git commit -m "PROP-061 git→FUSE detect+sync: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" 2>&1 | tail -1
  git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -1
fi
```
```
file_2_workspace_sync_md_changes.additional_removed_lines: Lines 143-157 (PRE_PULL_SHA / POST_PULL_SHA / PULL_MOVED capture in Step 1) — these become dead code post-PROP-066 since the helper script doesn't read them. Remove for clarity. Leaves Step 1 with just `git pull --rebase origin main` as it was pre-PROP-064.
file_2_workspace_sync_md_changes.net_delta: −157 +8 −15 = −164 lines net. workspace-sync.md goes from 1370 to ~1206 lines.

file_3_never_push_addition.additions: Add `'monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js'` to NEVER_PUSH (same as the other monitor/scripts/*.js entries). The script is source code, git-owned, edited via clone-and-push.
file_3_never_push_addition.ownership_in_build_js: Add `'monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js': 'git'` to OWNERSHIP in build.js (alongside existing script entries).

file_4_claude_md_changes.additions: Add `monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js` to the git-owned source-code list under the 'NEVER_PUSH source-code' subsection (alongside audit-rewrite.js, push-via-api.js, prune-integrity.js).
file_4_claude_md_changes.lessons_learned_addition: Add bullet: '(2026-05-31) **Step 4c helper-script extraction (PROP-066).** Complex bash blocks buried in long agent prompts are skip-prone. Even after PROP-064 added the PRE_PULL_SHA capture, Step 4c continued silent-skipping because (a) bash variables don't survive across MCP bash tool calls so PULL_MOVED was always 0 when Step 4c read it, and (b) the LLM observably skipped the 140-line Step 4c block on most cycles. Extracted to monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js; workspace-sync.md now invokes it via a 3-line bash block. The script derives PULL_MOVED equivalent from the last sync-workspace-runs sentinel timestamp (durable on-disk state), so it does not depend on Step 1''s bash variables. Always writes a sentinel for every cycle (success, divergence, abort, or no-op) so future audits can distinguish ran-and-decided-no-op from didn''t-run.'

idempotency_contract.script_invocation: Each invocation reads CURRENT origin/main state. If nothing has changed since the last sentinel, writes a no-op sentinel and exits. If invoked twice in the same minute, the second invocation's sentinel timestamp is identical (collision on filename); second invocation should detect and overwrite or skip — recommend overwrite (no harm; same content modulo timestamp ms).
idempotency_contract.sync_workspace_target: build.js sync-workspace is already idempotent (PROP-010): OWNERSHIP-whitelist copy, no delete logic. Running it on identical state is a no-op.
idempotency_contract.sentinel_writes: Sentinels are append-only (per-timestamp files). No mutation. PROP-051 retention sweep (prune-integrity) handles eventual cleanup.
idempotency_contract.git_operations: `git fetch` is idempotent. `git merge --ff-only` is conditional (fails cleanly if not FF). `git commit + push` are conditional on `git diff --cached --quiet`.

edge_cases.first_run_no_prior_sentinel: Helper script's 'last sentinel timestamp' fallback is hard-coded PROP-066-deployment-date. Use UTC ISO format. After deployment-date, the recursive 'most recent sentinel' check takes over.
edge_cases.concurrent_workspace_sync_runs: Cron design enforces non-concurrency (hourly, max 1 at a time). If a manual run overlaps a cron run, the helper script's `git pull --rebase` at Step 1 + `git push origin main` at the script's commit-and-push handles the race: one of them wins, the other gets a non-FF rejection on push (visible in stderr).
edge_cases.operator_force_push_to_origin: Detected by classification=true-non-ff. Script writes sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-<ts>.json and exits 1. Operator inspects, decides whether to manually `node build.js sync-workspace` after verifying canonical state. Same safety pattern as current Step 4c.
edge_cases.sync_workspace_build_js_target_missing: If build.js doesn't have the sync-workspace target (regression), `node build.js sync-workspace` exits non-zero with an error message captured in script's SYNC_OUT. Script writes sync-workspace-runs-<ts>.json with sync_exit_code=<nonzero> and output_tail=<error message>. Tinker Mode 2 catches this via the next health check.
edge_cases.script_itself_crashes_mid_execution: First-action sentinel (sync-workspace-step4c-started-<ts>.json) leaves a footprint. Process EXIT trap in script catches uncaught exceptions and writes sync-workspace-step4c-crash-<ts>.json with stack trace. Both are committed and pushed via the post-script bash block.
edge_cases.helper_script_pat_authentication_failure: Script invokes git CLI, which reads PAT from .git/config (set by Step 1 of workspace-sync). If PAT auth fails, git operations exit non-zero, caught by script's try/catch, written to crash sentinel. No silent failure.
edge_cases.scenarios_where_no_sentinel_would_have_b: Every workspace-sync cycle. Pre-PROP-066, ~94% of cycles wrote nothing. Post-PROP-066, every cycle writes at least one sentinel (action='no-op-no-upstream-movement' for the trivial steady-state). Sentinel write rate: ~1/hour × 24/day = 24/day. Prune-integrity's existing 30-day retention covers this with ~720 sentinels at steady-state — within the existing tinker-cost-engineering Mode 3 cleanup budget.

verification_path.synthetic_pre_deployment_test:
Run the helper script locally against a fresh clone with various conditions:
1. Fresh clone (no recent sentinels) → expect no-op sentinel with action='no-op-no-upstream-movement', sync_exit_code=0.
2. Make a commit to origin/main from a SECOND clone simulating an external agent → run the helper from the first clone → expect classification='equal' (after pull-and-fetch) but NEED_SYNC=1 because last-sentinel-timestamp < new-commit-timestamp; expect sync to fire; expect sync-workspace-runs sentinel with action='pull-moved-auto-sync'.
3. Make a local-only commit (don't push) → expect classification='remote-ancestor-of-local', NEED_SYNC=0, action='benign-local-ahead'.
4. Force-push a different commit from a SECOND clone, then run the helper from first clone → expect classification='true-non-ff', action='abort-non-ff', exit 1.
verification_path.real_world_post_deployment_canary: Within ≤1h of PROP-066 deployment, the next workspace-sync cycle writes ITS sentinel. Within 24h, expect 24 sentinels (one per hourly cycle), with the action distribution dependent on actual upstream activity. Most cycles: action='no-op-no-upstream-movement' or 'auto-sync'. Operator monitors via `ls monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-2026-06-* | wc -l` — expect ≥1/h.
verification_path.monitoring_dashboard: Add to tinker Mode 2 quick-check: count of sync-workspace-runs sentinels in last 24h. Expected ≥18 (allowing for ~6 cycles where workspace-sync itself was missed due to PAT/disk issues). If <18 for 2 consecutive tinker runs, flag as moderate-severity backlog-trend finding.
verification_path.the_failure_mode_distinction_we_can_fina: Pre-PROP-066: no sentinel → could mean 'Step 4c didn't run' OR 'Step 4c ran and correctly no-op'd'. Post-PROP-066: no sentinel → unambiguously means 'helper script wasn't invoked (workspace-sync's caller bash block was skipped)'. This is the diagnostic clarity that PROP-064 didn't deliver.

retrospective_on_prop_064_first_deployme.what_went_right: PROP-064's diagnosis was correct: PULL_MOVED IS the right trigger; the four-way classification IS the right discriminator; the structure-integrity FUSE-staleness audit IS the right canary.
retrospective_on_prop_064_first_deployme.what_went_wrong: PROP-064 assumed bash variable persistence across the LLM's bash tool calls. The Operational Notes at workspace-sync.md:168-170 explicitly document this gotcha for SKIP_LOG but PROP-064 did not apply the same pattern to PULL_MOVED. Secondary: PROP-064 left the silent-early-return at line 1232 — even successful Step 4c invocations write nothing for the modal 'equal+no-pull' case, making the success-vs-no-execution distinction impossible to draw from sentinels.
lessons_for_future_props: Bash variables do NOT cross MCP bash tool boundaries. If a value set in one Step needs to be read in a later Step, persist via tmpfile (SKIP_LOG pattern) OR derive freshly from on-disk state (PROP-066 pattern).

Long-prompt sections are LLM-skip-prone, especially when they appear between two natural-feeling completion points (Step 4b's push, Step 5's report). The remedy is structural: extract long bash blocks into helper scripts and invoke via 3-5 line shell calls.

Always-write-a-sentinel is a free observability win. The silent-early-return is a category of bug that conflates 'I ran and decided no-op' with 'I didn't run at all'. Future PROPs touching workspace-sync or other multi-step agents should default to writing a sentinel for every code path, including no-op.

phasing.phase_1_immediate: Author + commit sync-workspace-step4c.js + workspace-sync.md edits + build.js OWNERSHIP entry + CLAUDE.md updates in a single commit. Validate over 24h via the verification_path quick-check (sentinel count ≥18 in 24h). If validation passes, no further phases.
phasing.phase_2_followup_only_if_needed: If post-deployment sentinel rate is still <18/day, escalate to operator review. Possible follow-up: add a `monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-execution-counter.txt` counter (option_3) as a tertiary canary — but only if the helper-script extraction itself was insufficient.
phasing.rollback_plan: Revert the 4 files to their pre-PROP-066 state via git revert. Pre-PROP-066 behavior resumes within one workspace-sync cycle. Operator's manual sync-workspace remains as the always-available fallback. Total downtime: ≤1h.

estimated_effort.phase_1_implementation: ~60 min — 80 lines helper script (small but precise; needs the four-way logic + sentinel writes + crash handling), ~10 lines workspace-sync.md edit (delete 157 lines, add 8 lines, plus the Step 1 cleanup), ~3 lines build.js OWNERSHIP, ~10 lines CLAUDE.md updates.
estimated_effort.operator_review: ~15 min — read the helper script + diff the prompt change + scan CLAUDE.md update.

non_goals: Do NOT change build.js sync-workspace target behavior — operator-confirmed correct, idempotent, OWNERSHIP-whitelisted. The helper script invokes it as-is.
Do NOT modify smart_copy or any other Step 1/2/3/4a/4b/5 logic — out of scope.
Do NOT add new structure-integrity audits — PROP-064 Workstream C remains in flight and is independent.
Do NOT change the PROP-051 safety gates (A1-A4) — they protect different failure surfaces (mass-delete, disk pressure, working-tree underpopulation). Orthogonal to Step 4c.

Do NOT touch PROP-065's GIT_APPEND_ONLY deny-list — different vector, different protection. The helper script does not push individual files (workspace-sync's Step 2/4a does); PROP-065 protections continue to apply on those steps.

related_props.PROP-061: The original Phase-1+2 git→FUSE propagation Step 4c. PROP-066 supersedes its implementation while preserving the architecture.
related_props.PROP-064: The first attempt at fixing PROP-061's silent-skip. Diagnosis was correct, implementation was prompt-bash-bound; PROP-066 moves the same logic into a helper script that doesn't depend on bash-variable persistence.
related_props.PROP-010: build.js sync-workspace target. PROP-066's helper script invokes it as the actual file-copy mechanism — unchanged.
related_props.PROP-041_Phase_2_audit-rewrite.js: Precedent for helper-script extraction from prompt-bash. monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js was extracted from sloppytoppy-rewrite.md's bash for the same skip-by-omission + complex-logic reasons.
related_props.PROP-050_push-via-api.js: Precedent for helper-script extraction for git operations. Same scripts/ directory, same NEVER_PUSH + OWNERSHIP classification.
related_props.PROP-051_Workstream_C_prune-integrity.js: Precedent for retention/cleanup helper script. PROP-066's sentinels will accumulate; PROP-051's prune-integrity.js already covers monitor/integrity/ with appropriate retention windows.
related_props.PROP-065_GIT_APPEND_ONLY: Independent fix for a different vector (FUSE→git per-file overwrites). Composes cleanly with PROP-066: PROP-065 protects clone-and-push .jsonl files on the FUSE→git direction; PROP-066 ensures the git→FUSE direction reliably fires.

context_pointers.tinker_run_evidencing_the_gap: monitor/tinker/run-2026-05-31T02-40 FND-01 (5-file manual rescue) + run-2026-05-31T05-56 (this run, the diagnostic confirmation)
context_pointers.skip_log_persistence_precedent_PROP_064_: monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md lines 168-178 (mktemp the SKIP_LOG, no EXIT trap on the SKIP_LOG)

verification_pattern.for_tinker_followup_audit: 1) Confirm monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js exists in git and has appropriate executable permissions. 2) Confirm monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md no longer contains the lines 1210-1340 bash block; confirm it contains the new 3-line invocation. 3) Confirm build.js OWNERSHIP and CLAUDE.md include the new script. 4) Wait 24h post-deployment. 5) Run `ls monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-2026-*.json | wc -l` for cycles since deployment — expect ≥18 sentinels in 24h. ALL FIVE must pass for FIXED verdict. PARTIAL pass means a deployment regression — investigate which checkpoint failed.

estimated_blast_radius: New file (~80 lines), -157+8 lines in workspace-sync.md, +1 NEVER_PUSH entry, +1 build.js OWNERSHIP entry, +1 CLAUDE.md subsection bullet + 1 Lessons Learned bullet. Total LOC delta: ~60 lines reduction. Behavior change: every workspace-sync cycle now writes ≥1 sentinel under monitor/integrity/. Increased sentinel volume covered by existing prune-integrity retention. No producer agents touched. Risk of new bug: medium — the helper script has new logic, but the four-way classification is preserved verbatim from PROP-064's tested design; the new mechanism is just the PULL_MOVED-equivalent derivation from sentinel timestamps.
deployment_notes: Apply in this order: (1) author + commit sync-workspace-step4c.js (run synthetic_pre_deployment_test against it locally before committing); (2) update workspace-sync.md (delete the old block, add the new invocation, drop Step 1 dead code); (3) update build.js OWNERSHIP; (4) update CLAUDE.md. Single commit recommended for atomic deploy and easy revert. After deploy, monitor via verification_path.real_world_post_deployment_canary.
open_questions_for_operator: Should the helper script's 'last successful sync sentinel' fallback be hard-coded to '2026-05-30T00:00:00Z' (PROP-064 deployment), or read from a one-time config file (e.g. monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c-config.json)? Hard-code is simpler; config-file is more amendable. Recommend hard-code with a TODO comment to revisit if the script needs more configurability.

Should the script try to fall back to operator-readable plain-English errors in the crash sentinel, or keep the raw stack trace? Recommend raw stack trace + the input state (HEAD SHA, REMOTE SHA, last-sentinel-ts) so tinker can diagnose without re-running.

Phase 1's validation window is 24h. Acceptable? PROP-064 went 26h with one bootstrap success and then ~94% silent skip; want to avoid the same pattern. If PROP-066 shows <18 sentinels in the first 24h, escalate immediately to operator review rather than waiting another cycle.
